mechanism_narrative: Following an unprecedented shock that drives unemployment above 10%, any subsequent monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals the economic bottom is in. This triggers a forward-looking relief rally in equities as investors price in a nascent recovery, leading to positive returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 10% and its 1-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE value of 14.7 for April 2020 is a historical anomaly
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE falls from a peak above 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) is a direct, high-frequency measure of consumer spending momentum. Positive growth in consumption translates directly into higher expected corporate revenues, supporting higher equity prices over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2020 RSAFS showed a record monthly decline due to lockdowns
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 1-month percentage change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound (ZLB), monetary policy effectiveness shifts. In this regime, an increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) lowers real rates, eases private sector debt burdens, and signals robust enough demand to avoid a deflationary spiral, all of which are bullish for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut rates to 0-0.25% range in March 2020
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the correlation between 3-month changes in CPIAUCSL and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous recovery in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a broad-based, healthy economic restart. This joint signal of both supply-side activity and labor demand is a more robust indicator of future corporate earnings growth than either series in isolation, driving positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 1-month change of INDPRO > 0 and 1-month change of PAYEMS > 0
evidence_refs: fact:Both series saw historic collapses in April 2020
failure_condition: A model using the interaction of INDPRO and PAYEMS changes does not show a significantly better fit for predicting 60d returns than a model using only the additive effects of the two series.

mechanism_narrative: The absolute level of the unemployment rate serves as a direct measure of economic slack and distress. A higher level of unemployment indicates weaker aggregate demand and lower expected corporate earnings, exerting negative pressure on S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the level of UNRATE and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive or statistically insignificant over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a key forward-looking indicator of economic activity and big-ticket consumption. A sharp drop in housing starts below the critical 1 million annualized unit level signals a severe contraction and loss of confidence, triggering negative sentiment and short-term equity market declines.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing was a key leading indicator in the 2008 crisis
failure_condition: In months where HOUST is below 1,000, the average 5-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative and statistically different from the average return in all other months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising inflation (increasing input costs) and falling industrial production (weakening output) signals a stagflationary environment. This dynamic squeezes corporate profit margins from both the cost and revenue sides, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0.5% and 3-month change in INDPRO < -1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflation in the 1970s was highly detrimental to equity markets
failure_condition: Instances of rising CPI and falling INDPRO are not followed by statistically significant negative 60-day S&P 500 returns compared to other periods.

mechanism_narrative: After a major labor market shock (e.g., >1 million job losses), the first month with a positive change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) marks a critical turning point. This event signals the economic freefall has ceased, prompting a strong, positive, forward-looking response in the stock market.
candidate_trigger: 1-month change in PAYEMS > 0, following a month where the change was < -1000
evidence_refs: fact:April 2020 PAYEMS change was approximately -20,500 thousand
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following the first positive PAYEMS print after a major shock is not significantly greater than the unconditional average return (t-test p > 0.05).
